opportunities.title: The One-Page Lazy Prep Trick That Kills 90% of Session Prep
opportunities.why_now: Sly Flourish's fresh 'Lazy Campaigns' piece is prime raw material for a rules-ruling short that either validates or pushes back on his method.
opportunities.angle: 60-sec 'GM Ruling' short: show the actual one-page prep sheet on camera, dry-run one bullet against a real encounter, deliver a blunt verdict ('Works. Steal it.')

opportunities.title: Mike Shea Just Went Live — Here's the One Lazy DM Rule Everyone Steals Wrong
opportunities.why_now: Mike Shea (Sly Flourish) guested on EN World's podcast this week, giving a timely hook to react to his most-quoted lazy-DM technique.
opportunities.angle: Reaction-style short: pull one specific quote/technique from the livestream, run it through a live table example, correct the common misuse

opportunities.title: Running Clowns as Monsters: The Fear Ruling Most GMs Get Wrong
opportunities.why_now: EN World's 'Send in the Clowns' column dropped this week, giving a topical creature-design hook that's easy to turn into a stat-block/tactic short.
opportunities.angle: Creature tactic short: build a quick 'clown horror' stat block variant (fear save DC, disguise mechanic), demo how to run the reveal at the table

opportunities.title: Stealing Owlcat's New Psyker for Your 5e Table (No Homebrew Needed)
opportunities.why_now: Owlcat just revealed a psyker companion for Dark Heresy, giving a clean excuse to cover psionic/warp-magic reskins using existing 5e/Shadowdark spell frameworks.
opportunities.angle: Spell use-case short: take one psyker power (e.g., telepathic assault) and map it 1:1 onto an existing 5e spell or Shadowdark talent, with a ruling on backlash/side effects

opportunities.title: This Week's D&D News Digest Hid One Ruling You'll Use at Every Table
opportunities.why_now: The July 10 EN World news digest rounds up scattered rules chatter, giving a chance to cherry-pick the single most actionable nugget for a rapid-fire short.
opportunities.angle: Digest-mining format: skim the roundup on camera, pick the one crunchy rules item, turn it into a 30-second 'here's the actual ruling' clip
